intro: Franseria artemisioides, also known as California broomweed, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, white or pinkish flowers and a sprawling habit. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including the treatment of various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
